edges.description: SDH dysfunction blocks succinate oxidation and promotes succinate-driven inhibition of oxygen-sensing enzymes.
edges.source: Germline SDH complex loss of function

edges.description: Succinate also inhibits dioxygenases involved in DNA and histone demethylation.
Pseudohypoxia supports growth and survival of GIST and paraganglioma precursor cells.
Pseudohypoxic and epigenetic programs converge on tumor predisposition.
The SDH-deficient inherited tumor-predisposition mechanism produces the paraganglioma/pheochromocytoma arm of the Carney-Stratakis dyad.
The same SDH-deficient tumor-predisposition mechanism produces the gastric GIST arm of the syndrome.
Catecholamine-secreting paraganglioma or pheochromocytoma can produce episodic or sustained hypertension.
intermediate_mechanisms: Catecholamine secretion by paraganglioma or pheochromocytoma

edges.description: Gastric SDH-deficient GIST can present with severe epigastric pain or gastrointestinal bleeding.
intermediate_mechanisms: Gastric GIST with ulceration or bleeding

nodes.description: Gastric SDH-deficient GIST may present with abdominal pain, ulcer symptoms, or gastrointestinal bleeding, but presentation can be nonspecific.

evidence.reference_title: Current management of succinate dehydrogenase-deficient gastrointestinal stromal tumors.
evidence.snippet: Many patients with GIST are diagnosed after presenting with severe epigastric pain or gastrointestinal bleeding due to ulceration

nodes.description: Succinate accumulation inhibits alpha-ketoglutarate-dependent dioxygenases, including JmjC-domain histone demethylases, producing hypermethylation and altered differentiation programs in SDH-deficient tumors.

evidence.reference_title: Carney Triad, Carney-Stratakis Syndrome, 3PAS and Other Tumors Due to SDH Deficiency.
evidence.snippet: this acts as an alpha-ketoglutarate competitor, inhibiting a-KG-dependent dioxygenases, JIp1, which is involved in sulfur metabolism and Jhd1 which belongs to the JmjC-domain-containing histone demethylase (JHDM) enzymes.

nodes.description: SDH-deficient GIST is the other defining tumor class. Tumors are generally gastric, KIT/PDGFRA-wildtype, may be multifocal, and can recur or metastasize after long intervals.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical and molecular genetics of patients with the Carney-Stratakis syndrome and germline mutations of the genes coding for the succinate dehydrogenase subunits SDHB, SDHC, and SDHD.
evidence.snippet: In a subset of patients with GISTs, the lesions are associated with paragangliomas; the condition is familial and transmitted as an autosomal-dominant trait.

evidence.reference_title: Succinate dehydrogenase-deficient GISTs: a clinicopathologic, immunohistochemical, and molecular genetic study of 66 gastric GISTs with predilection to young age.
evidence.snippet: SDH-deficient GISTs constitute a small subgroup of gastric GISTs; they usually occur in children and young adults, often have a chronic course similar to that of pediatric and CT GISTs, and have potential association with paraganglioma, necessitating long-term follow-up.

nodes.description: Heterozygous germline pathogenic variants in SDHB, SDHC, or SDHD predispose to SDH-deficient tumors. Tumor suppressor behavior is supported by loss of function of the remaining allele in affected GIST tissue.
nodes.id: Germline SDH complex loss of function

biological_processes: mitochondrial electron transport, succinate to ubiquinone

evidence.reference_title: Clinical and molecular genetics of patients with the Carney-Stratakis syndrome and germline mutations of the genes coding for the succinate dehydrogenase subunits SDHB, SDHC, and SDHD.
evidence.snippet: We investigated 11 patients with the dyad of 'paraganglioma and gastric stromal sarcoma'; in eight (from seven unrelated families), the GISTs were caused by germline mutations of the genes encoding subunits B, C, or D (the SDHB, SDHC and SDHD genes, respectively).

evidence.reference_title: Carney Triad, Carney-Stratakis Syndrome, 3PAS and Other Tumors Due to SDH Deficiency.
evidence.snippet: CSS is caused by inactivating germline mutations in genes encoding for the SDH subunits, while CT is mostly caused by a specific pattern of methylation of the SDHC gene and may be due to germline mosaicism of the responsible genetic defect.

nodes.description: Catecholamine-secreting paragangliomas or pheochromocytomas may cause episodic or sustained hypertension, headache, palpitations, and sweating.

evidence.reference_title: Carney Triad, Carney-Stratakis Syndrome, 3PAS and Other Tumors Due to SDH Deficiency.
evidence.snippet: A patient with any type of PGL will present in any of the following contexts: a) because of signs and/or symptoms of excess catecholamine secretion (e.g. hypertension, headache, palpitations, hyperhidrosis, tremor);

nodes.description: SDH-deficient tumor biology predisposes to multifocal gastric GIST and multicentric paraganglioma or pheochromocytoma. GISTs are usually KIT/PDGFRA-wildtype, often gastric, and show loss of SDHB protein by immunohistochemistry.

evidence.snippet: The PGLs in CSS are multicentric and GISTs are multifocal in all the patients, suggesting an inherited susceptibility and associating the two manifestations.

evidence.reference_title: Succinate dehydrogenase-deficient GISTs: a clinicopathologic, immunohistochemical, and molecular genetic study of 66 gastric GISTs with predilection to young age.
evidence.snippet: SDH-deficient GISTs especially include pediatric GISTs and those associated with Carney triad (CT) or Carney-Stratakis syndromes (CSSs); the latter 2 also include paraganglioma as a component.

nodes.description: Paraganglioma, and less often pheochromocytoma, is one of the two defining tumor classes in the syndrome. Tumors may be multicentric and can occur in head and neck, retroperitoneal, adrenal, pelvic, or other paraganglionic sites.

evidence.reference_title: Bladder paraganglioma, gastrointestinal stromal tumor, and SDHB germline mutation in a patient with Carney-Stratakis syndrome: A case report and literature review.
evidence.snippet: BACKGROUND: Carney-Stratakis syndrome (CSS) is a rare dyad of paraganglioma (PGL)/pheochromocytoma (PHEO) and gastrointestinal stromal tumor (GIST).
CSS has autosomal dominant inheritance, incomplete penetrance, and greater relative frequency of PGL over GISTs.

evidence.snippet: the GISTs were caused by germline mutations of the genes encoding subunits B, C, or D (the SDHB, SDHC and SDHD genes, respectively).

nodes.description: Loss of SDH activity causes succinate accumulation. Succinate inhibits alpha-ketoglutarate-dependent prolyl hydroxylases, stabilizing HIF signaling under normoxic conditions and increasing angiogenic, glycolytic, and proliferative transcriptional programs.

evidence.reference_title: Current management of succinate dehydrogenase-deficient gastrointestinal stromal tumors.
evidence.snippet: SDH deficiency leads to accumulation of succinate, an oncometabolite that promotes tumorigenesis.

evidence.reference_title: Carney Triad, Carney-Stratakis Syndrome, 3PAS and Other Tumors Due to SDH Deficiency.
evidence.snippet: This mechanism implies that due to SDH deficiency, succinate is accumulated; this inhibits propyl hydroxylases (PHDs) resulting in induction of the hypoxic response despite normoxic conditions (pseudohypoxia)
